summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn preached a passionate message titled 'Lock the Doors,' drawing from the story of the widow and Elisha in 2 Kings 4. He emphasized that 2024 is a year for God's people to protect what God has given them by being spiritually vigilant. The central metaphor of locking doors represents guarding one's heart, mind, and life from the enemy's schemes. Pastor Travis taught that the devil prowls like a roaring lion, seeking any open door or unlocked window—often disguised as our greatest temptations—to steal, kill, and destroy. He called the congregation to be 'sober-minded' and alert, comparing believers to the Watchmen of the Old Testament who were always praying and warning of danger. The sermon highlighted the need to both build God's purposes and battle spiritual attacks, using the Word of God as our sword. Pastor Travis gave practical applications, urging listeners to audit what they allow into their minds, bodies, and relationships, and to decisively close doors to sin, fear, and compromise. He concluded with an invitation for salvation and a prayer for God to create clean hearts, reminding everyone that through Christ's blood, we are not our own but are called to honor God with our lives.
mainPoints: Believers must be spiritually alert and 'lock the doors' of their hearts and lives to protect against the enemy's schemes, which often come disguised as temptations.
We are called to be Watchmen—sober-minded, prayerful intercessors who build God's kingdom while being ready to battle spiritual attacks with the Word of God.
Honoring God requires guarding what enters our temple (body and mind) and refusing to give the devil a foothold by fleeing from evil and submitting to God.

versesMentioned.text: The wife of a man from the company of the prophets cried out to Elisha, 'Your servant my husband is dead, and you know that he revered the LORD. But now his creditor is coming to take my two boys as his slaves.' Elisha replied to her, 'How can I help you? Tell me, what do you have in your house?' 'Your servant has nothing there at all,' she said, 'except a small jar of olive oil.' Elisha said, 'Go around and ask all your neighbors for empty jars. Don’t ask for just a few. Then go inside and shut the door behind you and your sons. Pour oil into all the jars, and as each is filled, put it to one side.' She left him and shut the door behind her and her sons. They brought the jars to her and she kept pouring. When all the jars were full, she said to her son, 'Bring me another one.' But he replied, 'There is not a jar left.' Then the oil stopped flowing. She went and told the man of God, and he said, 'Go, sell the oil and pay your debts. You and your sons can live on what is left.'
Be alert and of sober mind. Your enemy the devil prowls around like a roaring lion looking for someone to devour.

The woman said to the serpent, 'We may eat fruit from the trees in the garden, but God did say, "You must not eat fruit from the tree that is in the middle of the garden, and you must not touch it, or you will die."' 'You will not certainly die,' the serpent said to the woman. 'For God knows that when you eat from it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing good and evil.'
I have posted watchmen on your walls, Jerusalem; they will pray day and night, continually. Take no rest, all you who pray to the LORD.
Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.

Do you not know that your bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit, who is in you, whom you have received from God? You are not your own; you were bought at a price. Therefore honor God with your bodies.
and do not give the devil a foothold.
Submit yourselves, then, to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.
I will give you a new heart and put a new spirit in you; I will remove from you your heart of stone and give you a heart of flesh.

Monday.scripture: 1 Peter 5:8 (NIV) - Be alert and of sober mind. Your enemy the devil prowls around like a roaring lion looking for someone to devour.
Monday.action: Identify one 'door' in your life you have left open to negative influence (e.g., a habit, a relationship, media consumption). Make a conscious decision to close it today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, open my eyes to see where I have been spiritually careless. Give me the courage and wisdom to close that door today. Help me to be alert to Your Spirit's leading.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 4:23 (NIV) - Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.
Tuesday.action: Perform a 'heart audit.' Write down the primary things you are allowing to influence your thoughts and emotions. Commit to removing one source of 'toxicity' or temptation.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, my heart is Yours. Show me what I have allowed in that does not honor You. Give me the strength to guard the gateway to my soul with Your truth.

Wednesday.scripture: Isaiah 62:6 (NLT) - I have posted watchmen on your walls, Jerusalem; they will pray day and night, continually.
Wednesday.action: Act as a Watchman for someone in your life. Spend 5 minutes in focused prayer for their spiritual protection and God's blessing over them.
Wednesday.prayer: God, thank You for calling me to pray. I stand in the gap for [name]. Surround them with Your protection, guide their steps, and let Your favor rest upon them.

Thursday.scripture: Ephesians 4:27 (NIV) - and do not give the devil a foothold.
Thursday.action: Reflect on a past failure where you gave the enemy an opportunity. In prayer, renounce that foothold and ask God to seal that area of your life with His grace.
Thursday.prayer: Lord, I confess I gave the enemy a place in my life when I [be specific in your heart]. I renounce that foothold now. By the blood of Jesus, I close that door. Fill that space with Your presence.

Friday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 6:19-20 (NIV) - You are not your own; you were bought at a price. Therefore honor God with your bodies.
Friday.action: Make one practical choice today that honors God with your body. This could be choosing healthy food, resting, speaking life, or avoiding compromising media.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, thank You for paying the ultimate price for me. My body is Your temple. Help me to honor You today in a tangible way with the choices I make for my physical and mental well-being.

Saturday.scripture: James 4:7 (NIV) - Submit yourselves, then, to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
Saturday.action: Practice active resistance. When a tempting or fearful thought arises, speak a scripture or declaration of truth out loud (e.g., 'I am submitted to God, so I resist that thought in Jesus' name').
Saturday.prayer: Almighty God, I submit every part of my life to You afresh. Your Word says when I resist the devil, he must flee. I stand on that promise today and declare my total dependence on You.
